clothingItems.name: Power Blazer
clothingItems.description: An oversized tailored blazer with pronounced shoulder pads and a longer line through the body.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Power dressing became a defining business look of the 1980s, especially in offices influenced by corporate ambition and gendered ideas of authority.

clothingItems.name: Stonewashed Denim Jacket
clothingItems.description: A medium-blue denim jacket with a boxy fit and stonewashed finish.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Stonewashed and acid-treated denim were common casual looks in the 1980s, especially for youth and weekend wear.

clothingItems.name: Nylon Track Jacket
clothingItems.description: A lightweight color-blocked nylon warm-up jacket with elastic cuffs.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Athletic warm-ups moved into street fashion in the 1980s and were especially visible in hip-hop, dance, and casual sportswear culture.

clothingItems.name: Satin-Lapel Dinner Jacket
clothingItems.description: A classic single-breasted black dinner jacket with satin lapels and a structured shoulder.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Formal eveningwear remained relatively conservative in the 1980s, though the decade often emphasized a stronger shoulder line.

clothingItems.name: Bow-Neck Silk Blouse
clothingItems.description: A soft silk blouse with a tie neck and slightly full sleeves.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Bow blouses paired well with suits and skirts in the 1980s office, balancing femininity with the decade's sharp tailoring.

clothingItems.name: Striped Oxford Shirt
clothingItems.description: A crisp striped button-front shirt in a slightly roomy fit.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Oxford shirts were a preppy and office-friendly staple in the 1980s, often tucked into pleated trousers or skirts.

clothingItems.name: Rock Band T-Shirt
clothingItems.description: A soft black concert t-shirt with a faded graphic and relaxed fit.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Band t-shirts were common markers of music identity in the 1980s across rock, metal, and punk scenes.

clothingItems.name: Satin Cocktail Dress
clothingItems.description: A knee-length cocktail dress with a fitted waist, sheen, and lightly padded shoulders.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: 1980s evening dresses often used shine, bright color, and strong shoulder emphasis rather than minimalism.

clothingItems.name: Argyle Sweater Vest
clothingItems.description: A knit argyle sweater vest worn over a collared shirt.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Preppy dressing remained influential in the 1980s, and sweater vests worked in both school-inspired and office-casual looks.

clothingItems.name: Pleated Trousers
clothingItems.description: High-waisted trousers with front pleats and a fuller leg.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Pleated trousers were common in 1980s office and formalwear, reflecting the decade's preference for volume and structure.

clothingItems.name: Knee-Length Pencil Skirt
clothingItems.description: A straight knee-length skirt in suiting fabric.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: The pencil skirt remained a standard companion to power blazers in 1980s businesswear.

clothingItems.name: Acid-Wash Jeans
clothingItems.description: High-waisted jeans with a tapered leg and bleached acid-wash finish.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Acid-wash denim is one of the clearest everyday denim markers of the 1980s.

clothingItems.name: Taffeta Party Skirt
clothingItems.description: A calf-length taffeta skirt with volume and a slight sheen.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Party and formal skirts in the 1980s often used crisp fabrics that held shape and caught light.

clothingItems.name: Pointed Pumps
clothingItems.description: Classic pointed pumps with a medium-to-high heel.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Pumps anchored both 1980s power dressing and formal eveningwear.

clothingItems.name: Leather Loafers
clothingItems.description: Structured leather loafers with a modest heel and clean vamp.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Loafers were practical for office, school, and preppy looks in the 1980s.

clothingItems.name: High-Top Basketball Sneakers
clothingItems.description: Leather high-top sneakers with bold panels and thick ankle padding.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Basketball sneakers became major fashion items in the 1980s through sport, hip-hop, and youth culture.

clothingItems.name: Open Plaid Flannel
clothingItems.description: An oversized plaid flannel shirt worn open as a light layer.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Flannel shirts became strongly associated with 1990s grunge and relaxed anti-fashion styling.

clothingItems.name: Oversized Denim Jacket
clothingItems.description: A loose, dropped-shoulder denim jacket in a light wash.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Oversized denim outerwear was common in 1990s casual dress across several youth styles.

clothingItems.name: Relaxed Single-Breasted Blazer
clothingItems.description: A softer blazer with reduced shoulder padding and a straighter cut.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Compared with the 1980s, 1990s tailoring generally moved toward less aggressive structure and cleaner lines.

clothingItems.name: Leather Car Coat
clothingItems.description: A hip-length black leather coat with a simple straight silhouette.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Leather coats and jackets appeared in many 1990s urban and minimalist wardrobes, especially in black.

clothingItems.name: Floral Babydoll Dress
clothingItems.description: A short floral dress with an empire waist and loose body.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Babydoll dresses were widely visible in 1990s alternative and youth fashion, often styled with boots.

clothingItems.name: Ribbed Tank Top
clothingItems.description: A fitted cotton rib tank with wide straps.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Simple ribbed tanks were a core 1990s basic, especially in pared-back casual wardrobes.

clothingItems.name: Black Mock-Neck Top
clothingItems.description: A close-fitting knit mock-neck top in black or another neutral.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Minimalist dressing in the 1990s favored sleek knit tops in dark neutrals, often paired with clean tailoring.

clothingItems.name: Rayon Camp Shirt
clothingItems.description: A boxy short-sleeve button-up in drapey rayon.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Camp shirts appeared in 1990s casual and vacation looks and could also read slightly dressier depending on styling.

clothingItems.name: Bias-Cut Slip Dress
clothingItems.description: A minimal slip dress with thin straps and fluid drape.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Slip dresses became an important 1990s formal and evening silhouette, reflecting the decade's stripped-back elegance.

clothingItems.name: Straight-Leg Trousers
clothingItems.description: Mid-rise trousers with a straight leg and clean front.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: 1990s workwear often softened from 1980s volume toward straighter, less padded silhouettes.

clothingItems.name: Bias-Cut Midi Skirt
clothingItems.description: A fluid midi skirt cut on the bias for a gentle cling and swing.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Bias skirts echoed the minimalist formal language of the 1990s and translated well into office wardrobes.

clothingItems.name: Baggy Jeans
clothingItems.description: Loose, long jeans with a wide leg and relaxed seat.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Baggy denim was central to 1990s skate and hip-hop influenced casual dress.

clothingItems.name: Khaki Chinos
clothingItems.description: Flat-front khaki chinos with a straight leg.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Chinos became a dependable mainstream casual and business-casual option in the 1990s.

clothingItems.name: Combat Boots
clothingItems.description: Chunky lace-up leather boots with a heavy sole.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Combat boots are one of the most recognizable 1990s markers, especially in grunge and alternative fashion.

clothingItems.name: Square-Toe Heels
clothingItems.description: Simple dress heels with a square toe and moderate heel height.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Square toes and cleaner, lower-profile heels fit the 1990s move away from overt 1980s excess.

clothingItems.name: Minimal Leather Loafers
clothingItems.description: Streamlined leather loafers with little ornament.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Minimal loafers suited the decade's pared-back office and smart casual looks.

clothingItems.name: Velour Track Jacket
clothingItems.description: A fitted zip-front velour track jacket with a plush surface.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Velour tracksuits became a highly visible 2000s casual look through celebrity culture and mall fashion.

clothingItems.name: Dark-Wash Trucker Jacket
clothingItems.description: A close-fitting dark-wash denim jacket with chest pockets and a shorter body.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Denim jackets persisted in the 2000s, often in darker washes and more fitted cuts than many 1990s versions.

clothingItems.name: Pinstripe Blazer
clothingItems.description: A slim pinstripe blazer with a nipped waist.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Early-2000s officewear often mixed tailored pieces with slimmer silhouettes and lower rises than previous decades.

clothingItems.name: Athletic Zip Jacket
clothingItems.description: A sporty zip jacket in technical knit with stripe detailing.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Logo-heavy athleticwear remained important in 2000s casual and youth dress, especially with sneaker culture.

clothingItems.name: Going-Out Halter Top
clothingItems.description: A fitted halter top with an open back and slight sheen.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: The halter was a signature early-2000s going-out top, often paired with low-rise denim or dressier pants.

clothingItems.name: Slim Polo Shirt
clothingItems.description: A fitted short-sleeve polo in pique cotton.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Polo shirts remained strongly present in 2000s mainstream casual dress, especially in mall brands and preppy looks.

clothingItems.name: Stretch Button-Front Shirt
clothingItems.description: A fitted stretch shirt designed to skim the torso and tuck into low-rise trousers or skirts.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Office and business-casual shirts in the 2000s often became more body-conscious than 1990s versions.

clothingItems.name: Logo Graphic T-Shirt
clothingItems.description: A fitted or baby tee with a large logo or slogan graphic.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Logo-heavy casual tops were a strong part of 2000s youth and street-influenced everyday fashion.

clothingItems.name: Strapless Party Dress
clothingItems.description: A short strapless dress with a body-skimming fit.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Strapless party dresses were common for 2000s dances, clubwear, and semi-formal social events.

clothingItems.name: Low-Rise Bootcut Jeans
clothingItems.description: Low-rise jeans fitted through the thigh and opening slightly at the hem.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Low-rise bootcut denim was one of the most characteristic mainstream silhouettes of the early 2000s.

clothingItems.name: Low-Rise Trousers
clothingItems.description: Tailored trousers cut lower on the waist with a longer lean leg.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Businesswear in the 2000s frequently adopted the decade's lower waistline and slimmer fit.

clothingItems.name: Denim Miniskirt
clothingItems.description: A short denim miniskirt in a fitted cut.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Denim minis were a highly visible 2000s casual item, often paired with boots, fitted tops, or layered looks.

clothingItems.name: Cargo Pants
clothingItems.description: Straight or loose cargo pants with flap utility pockets.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Cargo pants remained widespread in 2000s casualwear through skate, pop-punk, and mainstream utility trends.

clothingItems.name: Sheepskin Boots
clothingItems.description: Soft suede boots lined in sheepskin with a rounded toe and flat sole.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Sheepskin boots became a hallmark of 2000s casual dress, especially in celebrity-driven styling.

clothingItems.name: Chunky Skate Shoes
clothingItems.description: Padded skate shoes with a thick tongue and wide sole.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Chunky skate shoes were common in 2000s skate and youth fashion and crossed into mainstream casual wear.

clothingItems.name: Pointed-Toe Heels
clothingItems.description: Slim heels with an elongated pointed toe.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Pointed dress shoes stayed strong in 2000s office and formal wardrobes, often with slimmer, sharper lines.

clothingItems.name: Bomber Jacket
clothingItems.description: A lightweight bomber with ribbed cuffs, waistband, and collar.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Bomber jackets became a staple of 2010s streetwear and casual fashion across genders.

clothingItems.name: Longline Structured Blazer
clothingItems.description: A longline blazer with slim lapels and a cleaner, lightly oversized shape.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: The 2010s blended tailored workwear with softer length and less rigid construction than classic power suiting.

clothingItems.name: Stretch Denim Jacket
clothingItems.description: A fitted denim jacket with slight stretch and a streamlined body.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Denim jackets remained dependable basics in the 2010s, often cut slimmer than 1990s versions.

clothingItems.name: Open-Front Cardigan
clothingItems.description: A soft draped cardigan worn open over basics.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Long cardigans were common in 2010s casual and business-casual wardrobes, especially in layered minimal looks.

clothingItems.name: Peplum Blouse
clothingItems.description: A fitted blouse that flares slightly at the waist into a peplum.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Peplum tops were a recognizable early-2010s silhouette in office and dressier settings.

clothingItems.name: Chambray Shirt
clothingItems.description: A soft chambray button-front shirt in a relaxed everyday fit.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Chambray shirts became a common 2010s casual staple, especially in layered and smart-casual wardrobes.

clothingItems.name: Fitted Graphic T-Shirt
clothingItems.description: A slim or straight-fit t-shirt with a clean graphic print.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Graphic tees remained central in 2010s casual dress, often paired with slim denim, sneakers, or bomber jackets.

clothingItems.name: Sheath Dress
clothingItems.description: A knee-length sheath dress with a close fit and clean seaming.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: The sheath dress remained a practical 2010s business and event option, especially in polished professional wardrobes.

clothingItems.name: Athleisure Tank
clothingItems.description: A sleek performance-inspired tank suitable for both workouts and everyday wear.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Athleisure became one of the defining fashion shifts of the 2010s, blurring activewear and everyday dress.

clothingItems.name: Skinny Jeans
clothingItems.description: Stretch jeans cut close from hip to ankle.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Skinny jeans dominated mainstream casualwear through much of the 2010s for multiple genders.

clothingItems.name: Cigarette Trousers
clothingItems.description: Slim ankle-length trousers with a clean flat front.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Slim ankle trousers fit the 2010s preference for neat, shortened, body-conscious tailoring.

clothingItems.name: Jogger Pants
clothingItems.description: Tapered knit or woven joggers finished with elastic cuffs.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Joggers translated athletic cues into mainstream streetwear and casual fashion in the 2010s.

clothingItems.name: Pleated Midi Skirt
clothingItems.description: A midi skirt with soft pleats and fluid movement.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Midi lengths returned strongly in the 2010s and worked across professional, modest, and dressy everyday looks.

clothingItems.name: Minimal White Sneakers
clothingItems.description: Clean low-top white sneakers with minimal branding.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Simple white sneakers became one of the most versatile 2010s footwear staples.

clothingItems.name: Chelsea Ankle Boots
clothingItems.description: Sleek ankle boots with elastic side gores and a low heel.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Chelsea boots were widely worn in the 2010s across smart-casual, office, and evening looks.

clothingItems.name: Block-Heel Sandals
clothingItems.description: Open or closed-toe sandals with a sturdy block heel.
clothingItems.historicalNotes: Block heels offered the 2010s balance of trendiness and practicality for dressier wear.
